title: CGM Sensor Assembly
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 1 — International CGM manufacturer establishing Saudi sensor assembly for Middle East market
executive_summary:
Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) sensors — wearable biosensors that measure blood glucose levels in real-time for diabetic patients — are the highest-growth segment of the diabetes device market. Saudi Arabia has one of the world's highest diabetes prevalence rates: approximately 18% of adults, representing 4–5 million diabetic patients. Saudi Arabia is also one of the world's largest CGM markets by per-capita diabetes burden, yet all CGM sensors are imported from Dexcom, Abbott (FreeStyle Libre), and Medtronic.

CGM sensor manufacturing combines electrochemical sensor fabrication (glucose-sensitive enzyme coatings on electrodes), wireless electronics (Bluetooth), and biocompatible adhesive assembly — manufacturing processes that are technically accessible with appropriate standards and clean-room infrastructure. Node MD-6 targets Saudi assembly of CGM sensors for the Middle East market, capturing the manufacturing value-add from Saudi Arabia's massive domestic demand.

The global CGM market was valued at $6.8 billion in 2024 growing at 24.2% CAGR — one of the highest growth rates in medical devices. Saudi Arabia's domestic CGM market is estimated at $350–500 million annually. A Saudi assembly facility serving the GCC (where diabetes prevalence is similarly high) would access a $800M–1.2B annual market.

companies.name: Abbott (FreeStyle Libre)
companies.rationale: Abbott's FreeStyle Libre has the largest Middle East CGM market share. Abbott has manufacturing partners internationally and Saudi Arabia is its largest CGM market outside the US and Europe.
companies.contact: Abbott Diabetes Care VP International Manufacturing

companies.name: Dexcom
companies.rationale: Dexcom is the premium CGM manufacturer with active Saudi Arabia government procurement. Saudi assembly under Dexcom licence.

companies.name: Medtronic Diabetes
companies.rationale: Medtronic has integrated CGM and insulin pump systems with Saudi Arabia hospital relationships.

companies.name: Roche Diabetes Care
companies.rationale: Roche has diabetes monitoring devices and active Saudi Arabia market presence.
companies.contact: Roche Diabetes Care VP International Manufacturing

companies.name: iSens (South Korea)
companies.rationale: iSens produces CGM sensors with lower technology barriers and active international partnerships. Entry-level CGM for Saudi assembly.

partners.name: NUPCO / Saudi Diabetes Association
partners.current: CGM procurement for Saudi government hospitals; 4+ million diabetic patients
partners.upgrade: NUPCO 5-year offtake agreement for Saudi-assembled CGM sensors; Saudi Diabetes Association clinical endorsement; anchor procurement enabling Abbott or Dexcom assembly JV

partners.name: SFDA
partners.current: Class III implantable device regulation; in vitro diagnostic oversight
partners.upgrade: SFDA CGM manufacturing licence pathway; Saudi clinical validation framework; ISO 13485 fast-track for Saudi CGM assembler

partners.name: KAUST Bioengineering
partners.current: Biosensor research; electrochemical sensing; biocompatible materials
partners.upgrade: GADD-KAUST CGM sensor research; Saudi-specific glucose calibration algorithm (population-specific factors); next-generation CGM research

partners.name: Saudi Electronics Companies
partners.current: PCB assembly; Bluetooth electronics; IoT device assembly
partners.upgrade: Medical-grade electronics assembly (ISO 13485); CGM wireless electronics sub-assembly; biocompatible adhesive application processes
